expectations.text: Contains all 10 PRD sections (Executive Summary through Open Questions)
expectations.evidence: All 10 sections present: Section 1 (Executive Summary), Section 2 (Business Context), Section 3 (User Requirements), Section 4 (Functional Requirements), Section 5 (Non-Functional Requirements), Section 6 (Technical Architecture Notes), Section 7 (Go-to-Market Requirements), Section 8 (Launch Plan), Section 9 (Dependencies and Risks), Section 10 (Open Questions), plus Appendices.

expectations.text: Success metrics include a failure threshold
expectations.evidence: Section 2 includes: 'Failure threshold: If fewer than 10% of active workspaces create a workflow within 6 months, or if workflow execution reliability falls below 98%, we will re-evaluate the approach'

expectations.text: Uses named personas from product.local.md, not 'users' or 'a user'
expectations.evidence: Uses named personas 'Ops Olivia' (Operations Manager) and 'Dev Darius' (Platform Engineer). Noted product.local.md not found and personas are illustrative, which is appropriate behavior when config is missing.

expectations.text: Functional requirements use MoSCoW prioritisation with no more than 60% MUST
expectations.evidence: Section 4 uses MoSCoW: 4 MUST (50%), 2 SHOULD (25%), 2 COULD (25%). Explicitly states 'Within the 60% MUST ceiling.'

expectations.text: Technical architecture notes section is present
expectations.evidence: Section 6 present with detailed architecture notes including key technical decisions, platform dependencies, third-party integrations, data model changes, migration requirements, and feature flags. Includes engineering review gate note.

expectations.text: PRD status is marked as DRAFT
expectations.evidence: Header shows '**Status**: DRAFT' and first line includes 'VERSION: Draft'

expectations.text: Go-to-market section addresses documentation, CS enablement, and sales enablement
expectations.evidence: Section 7 includes Documentation (user guide, action reference, API reference), Customer Success Enablement (briefing, sandbox, FAQ, training), Sales Enablement (demo script, competitive comparison, talking points), plus Pricing and Beta Programme.

expectations.text: Non-functional requirements include performance targets with specific numbers
expectations.evidence: Section 5 Performance: '< 5 seconds (p95)', '< 2 seconds on broadband', '< 500ms response', '30 seconds per action step'. Reliability: '99.9%', '< 15 minutes RTO'. Scalability: '1,000 concurrent active workflows', '500 events/second'.
